text:
In a tiny, charming village nestled between rolling hills and whispering woods, there lived a gnome named Gerben. Gerben was known far and wide not just for his delightful disposition but also for his incredible passion for collecting antique buttons. His tiny cottage brimmed with an eclectic mix of buttons from around the globe; tiny polka dots, elaborate floral designs, ancient-looking beasts, and even a few buttons that looked like tiny birds.

One crisp autumn morning, as Gerben sat admiring his collection under an old oak tree, a gust of wind swept through. His bird button fluttered away. Panicking, Gerben peered under the tree. Just there—a tiny, ancient-looking button, woven with threads that shimmered like moonlight. He couldn’t decide which animal it resembled. Could it be a hedgehog? A quail?

That evening, Gerben decided he had to find the bird button again. He donned his nicest cloak—darned if it wasn
